title: hyacinthus orientalis subsp. albulus
intro: Hyacinthus orientalis subsp. albulus, commonly known as the white hyacinth or white hyacinth, is a subspecies of the hyacinth plant, characterized by its white flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of the white hyacinth include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of the white hyacinth can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and well-being.
